message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To assess the performance of antigen-based rapid diagnostic tests (Ag-RDTs) for SARS CoV-2 when implemented for large-scale universal screening of asymptomatic individuals.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study presents data from a pragmatic implementation study for universal Ag-RDT-based screening at a tertiary care hospital in Germany where all incoming patients without symptoms suggestive of SARS-CoV-2 were screened with an Ag-RDT prior to admission since October 2020.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Result</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In total, 49,542 RDTs were performed in 27,199 asymptomatic individuals over a duration of five months. Out of 222 positive results, 196 underwent in-house confirmatory testing with PCR, out of which 170 were confirmed positive, indicating a positive predictive value (PPV) of 86.7%. Negative Ag-RDTs were not routinely tested with PCR, but a total of 94 cases of false negative Ag-RDTs were detected due to PCR tests being performed within the following five days with a median CT-value of 33.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>This study provides evidence that Ag-RDTs can have a high diagnostic yield for transmission relevant infections with limited false-positives when utilized at the point of care on asymptomatic patients and thus can be a suitable public-health test for universal screening.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: The potential of SARS-CoV-2 antigen-detection tests in the screening of asymptomatic persons

reference.unstructured: World Health Organization. SARS-CoV-2 antigen-detecting rapid diagnostic tests: an implementation guide 2020. Available at: https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240017740 Accessed April 28, 2021.

reference.article-title: Rolling out COVID-19 antigen rapid diagnostic tests: The time is now
Rapid, point-of-care antigen and molecular-based tests for diagnosis of SARS-CoV-2 infection

reference.unstructured: Brümmer LE , Katzenschlager S , Gaeddert M , et al. The accuracy of novel antigen rapid diagnostics for SARS-CoV-2: A living systematic review and meta-analysis. medRxiv 2021:2021.02.26.21252546.

reference.article-title: SARS-CoV-2 testing for public health use: Core principles and considerations for defined use settings

reference.unstructured: Heidelberg University Hospital. Annual Report 2019 2020. Available at: https://report.ukhd-mfhd.de/2019/ Accessed April 28, 2021.
Kissler SM , Fauver JR , Mack C , et al. SARS-CoV-2 viral dynamics in acute infections 2020.
